roles.48598: triggering alert: reg add Winlogon /v DefaultUserName /d administrator — writing plaintext AutoLogon credentials to the registry (T1552.002)
roles.48607: reg add Winlogon /v DefaultPassword /d 12345 — the plaintext password value
roles.48813: EID12: DefaultPassword value DELETED (regedit cleanup) — the credential is removed shortly after

metadata.security_proposition: The observed AutoLogon registry writes are adversary-driven credential exposure or theft.

attack_category.category: Credentials in registry: AutoLogon set then reverted
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = WEAK. AutoLogon username and password values are written to Winlogon. A later event shows DefaultPassword deletion, but deletion of DefaultUserName is not evidenced. No read, use, exfiltration, or authentication follow-on is observed. Intent remains ambiguous. Do not leak to model.

boundary_definition: A0 = the DefaultUserName write; EV = the DefaultPassword write, the two EID13 sets, and the EID12 delete (revert).
No credential read/exfil/use — outcome absent; the value is reverted.

curation_notes.note: WEAK / over-triage-prone: AutoLogon values are written and DefaultPassword is later deleted; no read, use, exfiltration, or authentication follow-on is evidenced.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
